text.title: Traceability Tooling Comparison (Jira / ADO / Polarion) Worked Example
text.summary: One end-to-end worked example for Traceability Tooling Comparison (Jira / ADO / Polarion). Shows the artefact filled against a realistic input set.

section.title: Scenario
section.p: A pro-tier operator is running the recurring activity tied to Traceability Tooling Comparison (Jira / ADO / Polarion). The named consumer is the engagement owner; inputs are pinned to the operator's source-of-truth (engagement file, ticket tracker, decision log). The operator applies the procedure and produces a versioned artefact.

list.item: Triggering activity: recent instance within the last 30 days, recorded in the engagement file.
Prior cycle's artefact (if any) for trend comparison.
Step 1 — preconditions check passes; Applies-If satisfied, no Skip-If triggers.
Step 2 — three inputs gathered and pinned with stable paths.
Step 3 — every rule from 01-core-rules walked; conformance recorded; one rule initially fails (defensive rationale) and is repaired by adding an input citation.
Step 4 — template under templates/ filled; output validates against the JSON Schema from 02-output-contract.
Step 5 — validator exits 0; artefact handed to the named consumer with a "ready for owner review" flag.

section.title: Result
section.p: The named consumer signs off in < 1 review cycle because the artefact is owner-named, input-cited, and schema-valid. The artefact is versioned 1.0.0 with last_reviewed = 2026-05-23; the next refresh will bump version per the change-class rule.
